assertions.title: Out-of-order terminal signals cannot regress active flow

steps.expected: All tests pass including stale signal suppression tests
steps.observed: 299 tests passed, including comprehensive stale signal fencing tests

steps.expected: run_completed with mismatched runSeq is ignored even when runId matches
steps.observed: Test confirms: activeRunSeq 7 vs event runSeq 8 -> no transition, version unchanged

steps.action: Verify runId mismatch handling in implementing state
steps.expected: Stale runId events are fenced and ignored
steps.observed: All 4 fence tests pass: run_completed, dispatch_acked, run_failed, dispatch_ack_timeout with different runId are ignored

steps.action: Verify headSha correlation for CI gates
steps.expected: Stale CI verdicts with mismatched headSha are ignored
steps.observed: Test confirms: headSha 'sha-current' vs event 'sha-stale' -> no transition

steps.expected: Terminal states (done, stopped, terminated) absorb all events without regression
steps.observed: All terminal absorption tests pass - no state regression possible from terminal states

steps.expected: Older runSeq values cannot cause regressions
steps.observed: awaiting_pr_creation ignores gate_review_failed with stale runSeq (3 vs active 4)

keyTestCases: implementing run_completed with mismatched runSeq is ignored even when runId matches
gate_review_passed with mismatched runSeq is ignored
gate_ci_passed with mismatched runSeq is ignored even when headSha matches
implementing state fences stale runIds (4 test cases)
ignores stale CI verdicts with mismatched headSha
awaiting_pr_creation ignores gate_review_failed from a stale runSeq
terminal states absorb all events (done, stopped, terminated)

summary: Tested VAL-CROSS-003: All stale signal suppression behaviors verified. The reducer correctly fences on runId, runSeq, and headSha to prevent out-of-order terminal signals from regressing active flow. 299 tests passed including comprehensive coverage of terminal state absorption and stale event suppression.
